reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author is asking for clarification on whether an additional reference leak exists, specifically regarding the afs_add_open_mmap function, and if so, which of two functions should be removed.

Author acknowledged that the afs_add_open_mmap function can cause a reference leak, agreed to remove it and keep only the one in afs_mapped(), indicating a fix is planned for v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Is the above afs_add_open_mmap an additional one, which could cause a reference
leak? Does the above one need to be removed and only the one in afs_mapped()
needs to be kept?

---

reviews.author: Lorenzo (Oracle)
reviews.summary: Reviewer questioned whether the additional afs_add_open_mmap call in this patch is necessary, suggesting it could cause a reference leak and asking if it should be removed to avoid duplication of code
reviews.raw_body:
Cheers, Lorenzo

______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch fixes a potential memory leak issue in the AFS (Andrew File System) by ensuring that the reference count on a mapping is correctly dropped when there's an error, preventing a reference leak.
